title: Buffer overflow in Curl_urldecode()
source: Inspired by Daniel Stenberg's 'Death by a thousand slops' blog post (Dec 2025): 19 of 20 bug-bounty reports to curl were AI hallucinations
security_summary: CRITICAL: Hallucinated vulnerability. Function Curl_urldecode() does not exist in current curl codebase. The proposed buffer arithmetic is safe and the suggested overflow does not reproduce. CordonEnforcer isolates this finding from the synthesizer verdict.

layers.summary: Boilerplate narrative comment: 'This function does X.'
TODO stub masquerading as a real fix: 'refactor into smaller helpers'
CRITICAL: Hallucinated vulnerability. Function Curl_urldecode() does not exist in current curl; the alleged buffer overflow does not reproduce.
Bypasses the existing url.c parse pipeline (Curl_url_set) and reinvents URL handling locally
Risk 0.92 / 1.00 -- Halted. CordonEnforcer isolates the hallucinated vuln. No comment posted; no labels set.

title: feat: add Stripe checkout integration
source: Classic AI slop pattern: hardcoded API key + defensive .clone() everywhere + 'This function does X' boilerplate
security_summary: CRITICAL (CWE-798): Hardcoded Stripe live secret key in source. CWE-200: response.clone() exposes payment intent data in memory. Must move to env var + secret manager; do not commit.

layers.summary: Boilerplate 'This function does X' doc-comment on a one-liner
layers.diff_range: +// This function does X: it creates a checkout session.

layers.summary: CRITICAL (CWE-798): Hardcoded Stripe live secret key committed to source
WARNING (CWE-200): response.clone() retains full payload in memory unnecessarily
Bypasses the existing payment-provider abstraction (PaymentProvider interface) and hardcodes Stripe
Risk 0.97 / 1.00 -- Halted. Must move secret to env var + secret manager; do not merge.

title: refactor: simplify ts.transform signature
source: Defensive .clone() everywhere + boilerplate 'This function does X' / 'We need to' narrative comments; classic LLM verbose-cargo pattern
security_summary: No security regressions. SLOP signals: 4 boilerplate doc-comments, 3 defensive .clone() calls, 1 ignored return value (.clone() in for-loop body). Deterministic layer caught all 4 comments in 8ms.

title: feat: add keyboard shortcut to duplicate selection
source: Modeled on Steve Ruiz's 'Stay away from my trash' blog: 'We need to' boilerplate + defensive .clone() + tautological assertions in test files
security_summary: No security regressions. SLOP signals: 5 'We need to' / 'This function does X' boilerplate comments, 4 defensive .clone() calls (none required by Editor contract), 1 tautological assertion (SLOP-EVASION-007: assert.equal(true, true)).

layers.summary: Boilerplate 'We need to' narrative comments (4 instances)
layers.diff_range: +// We need to add a keyboard shortcut for duplicating the current selection.

layers.summary: Defensive .clone() chain (4 calls); Editor.selectedShapes is already immutable
Tautological assertion in test (SLOP-EVASION-007): assert.equal(true, true)
INFO: keyboard shortcut should be wired in the existing useKeyboardShortcuts hook, not a free-floating function

Risk 0.50 / 1.00 -- ReviewRequired. Strip 'We need to' comments; drop .clone() chain; replace tautological assertion with a real expectation (e.g. expect(editor.selectedShapes.length).toBe(2 * initial)).
